title: Portulaca catharinae
intro: Portulaca catharinae, also known as the Catharanthus plant, is a flowering succulent native to Madagascar, belonging to the Portulacaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of infections. The bioactive constituents of Portulaca catharinae include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Portulaca catharinae is classified within the family Portulacaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as purslanes. This family contains around 30-40 genera and 500-600 species, with Portulaca catharinae being one of them. Portulaca catharinae belongs to the genus Portulaca, which is native to tropical regions of the world. The genus Portulaca is further classified within the order Caryophyllales, which includes various flowering plant species. The species Portulaca catharinae is native to tropical regions of the Americas and is also known as the 'Rose Moss'.
uses: Portulaca catharinae has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antioxidant and antifungal properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. In traditional medicine, Portulaca catharinae is used to treat fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. The plant is also used in some cultures as a treatment for wounds and infections.

preparations: Portulaca catharinae herbal preparations are not well-documented, but some sources suggest that the plant has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
